sanskrit:
अथ केन प्रयुक्तोऽयं पापं चरति पूरुषः।
अनिच्छन्नपि वार्ष्णेय बलादिव नियोजितः॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
atha kena prayukto ’yaṁ pāpaṁ carati pūruṣaḥ
anicchann api vārṣṇeya balād iva niyojitaḥ
literal_meaning: Arjuna said: Then, by what is this man impelled to commit sin, O descendant of Vṛṣṇi, even against his will, as if forced by strength?
contextual_meaning: Arjuna asks why people act against their own values, feeling compelled toward harmful choices as if an external force overrides their will.
psychological_state: ego-dystonic guilt—behaviors clash with self-image, producing confusion about autonomous control
trigger_scenario: After vowing to quit doom-scrolling or binge-eating, you relapse at 2 a.m., feeling hijacked by an inner autopilot
anchor_text: Why do we act against our own will, as if forced?
